data.title: Holmes Beach saw a 10% rise in short-term rental compliance in 2025, with a partnership with Deckard Technologies driving the increase. 1,600 short-term rentals were monitored.
data.description: In Holmes Beach, Florida, short term rental compliance saw a significant 10% boost in 2025. This improvement came after city officials collaborated with Deckard Technologies to revamp oversight and enforcement measures. The partnership led to a more efficient monitoring system. Over 1,600 short term rentals were involved in this initiative to enhance adherence to regulations.

data.title: Software stocks and silver prices rise: IGV ETF jumps 3%, silver rallies 6% to $82. Market rebound with Nasdaq up 1%, S&P 500 up 0.7% on Monday. Oracle Corp. soars 11%, AppLovin Corp. surges 14%, and Palantir Technologies Inc. rises 7% in best sessions since 2025. Dow Jones nears record high at 50,100. Oil prices also see positive movement.
data.description: Tech software sector stocks continued their recovery for a second day following a significant drop last week. The iShares Expanded Tech-Software Sector ETF (NYSE: IGV) surged over 3% on Monday, marking its strongest daily increase since April 2025. In contrast, the Nasdaq 100 saw a 1% rise, outperforming the broader market, while the S&P 500 showed a 0.7% increase. Small cap stocks also climbed by 1%, while the Dow Jones Industrial Average remained relatively stable near the 50,100 mark, just under its all-time high. Among software companies, AppLovin Corp. (NASDAQ: APP) surged by over 14% after facing four consecutive weeks of declines. Oracle Corp. (NYSE: ORCL) had an impressive 11% jump, its best performance since September, and Palantir Technologies Inc. (NASDAQ: PLTR) rose by 7%, extending its 4.5% gain from Friday. Additionally, commodities experienced an increase in line with the overall positive market sentiment.

data.title: Engels Gualdani in Hilton, NY advises on classic car safety upgrades in HelloNation article.
data.description: In a recent article by HelloNation, Engels Gualdani from Great Lakes Classic Cars in Hilton, NY discussed ways for owners to enhance safety in a vintage vehicle while preserving its original aesthetics. The focus was on upgrading critical parts without compromising the classic style. This guidance aimed to address the growing concern for safety in classic cars.

data.title: In today's jobs report, 130,000 jobs were added in January 2026. However, 898,000 jobs were later erased from 2025's totals. Unemployment rate decreased to 4.3%. Headline job numbers often mislead as they are based on estimates. The real figures take months to be revealed, causing uncertainties in financial decisions. Fed rate-cut bets shifted from June to July based on the misleading job numbers. The headline figure is often inaccurate and based on guesswork, making it unreliable for market movements. Most people are unaware of the discrepancies between the headline figure and the revised, more accurate numbers months later.
data.description: The Bureau of Labor Statistics releases monthly jobs reports with the headline nonfarm payrolls figure, which can often be inaccurate and misleading due to its reliance on estimates and the "birth-death model." The January 2026 report showed 130,000 jobs added, with unemployment at 4.3% and strong health care hiring. However, the BLS later revealed that nearly 900,000 jobs from 2025's data were erased due to inaccuracies in the model. The headline number is based on a survey of around 119,000 businesses covering only 15% of all workers, with the rest extrapolated through statistical methods and guesses. The birth-death model further skews the data with guesses on new and closing businesses, leading to large potential swings in reported job growth. The BLS's own technical notes admit a 90% confidence range of plus or minus 136,000 jobs, showing the uncertainty in the reported numbers. The article highlights the importance of understanding the limitations of the headline jobs report figures and the need to consider the revisions based on real data for a more accurate assessment of the job market trends.

data.title: Software sector slump sees iShares Tech-Expanded Software Sector ETF (NYSE: IGV) plunging 3%. Bitcoin drops to $66,000 amidst Wednesday market movements. Palantir, Salesforce, Intuit, and ServiceNow among big names experiencing losses. U.S. equity indexes show modest gains despite mixed signals from labor market - January jobs report exceeds expectations but faces downward revisions for future growth.
data.description: The software selloff continued as the iShares Tech-Expanded Software Sector ETF (IGV) slid over 3% on Wednesday, with only five out of more than 100 constituents managing to avoid losses. Large-cap names like Palantir Technologies Inc. (PLTR), Salesforce Inc. (CRM), Intuit Inc. (INTU), and ServiceNow Inc. (NOW) each dropped around 5%. The January jobs report showed nonfarm payrolls rising by 130,000, surpassing economists' expectations of 70,000, with private-sector employment increasing by 172,000. Despite the positive headline numbers, there were sweeping downward revisions, slashing total U.S. job growth for 2025 to just 181,000. Unemployment rate unexpectedly declined to 4.3% from 4.4%.

data.title: Stock market indexes hold gains after CPI data report, with a vaccine stock rising; power name hits new high. Dow and major indexes remain positive.
data.description: Today, the stock market saw major indexes maintaining a positive trend after some fluctuations earlier in the day. Particularly, a vaccine-related stock experienced a significant surge. The Dow is holding onto a gain following the release of CPI data, indicating a stable performance. Additionally, a prominent company reached a new high, reflecting optimism in the market.

data.title: Real estate expert Robin Cavallaro helps military families navigate PCS home buying in Annapolis, MD for Southern Maryland, with tips on housing process.
data.description: In Annapolis, Maryland, a recent article by HelloNation provides guidance for military families preparing for a Permanent Change of Station (PCS) to the area. The piece offers insights on the housing process for families moving to Southern Maryland, Annapolis, and nearby regions. It outlines key considerations and tips for those relocating, aiming to ease the transition. The article discusses important aspects such as accommodation options, neighborhood insights, school information, and other essential factors for military families. By highlighting these details, HelloNation aims to support military families during their relocation process to Maryland.

data.title: EPC and Renesas sign agreement for GaN tech licensing, Renesas to access EPC's eGaN tech, accelerating high-voltage adoption.
data.description: In El Segundo, California, Efficient Power Conversion (EPC) has entered a significant licensing deal with Renesas Electronics Corporation, a leading provider of advanced semiconductor solutions. This agreement grants Renesas access to EPC's renowned low-voltage eGaN technology and supply-chain network, aimed at speeding up the integration of high-voltage GaN transistors. EPC is recognized as a global leader in enhancement-mode gallium nitride (eGaN) power devices, while Renesas is a premier supplier of semiconductor solutions. This partnership is set to drive innovation and promote the adoption of cutting-edge technology in the industry, benefiting both companies and customers alike.

data.title: White House celebrates amid robust economic numbers: strong jobs data and low inflation in January are positive for President Trump.
data.description: Positive job growth numbers for January have brightened President Trump's outlook, although inflation figures remain subdued. Despite these promising updates, the overall economic landscape presents a less optimistic view.

data.title: Taseko Mines Limited to release Q4 and year end 2025 results on Feb 18, 2026 after market close.
data.description: In Vancouver, British Columbia, on February 13, 2026, Taseko Mines Limited (TSX: TKO; NYSE American: TGB; LSE: TKO) announced that it will unveil its financial results for the fourth quarter and full year of 2025 on Wednesday, February 18, 2026, after the market closes.

data.title: Passage of Clarity Act "comfort" markets amid Bitcoin volatility, says Treasury Sec Bessent.
data.description: The passage of the Clarity Act, as suggested by U.S. Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent, is expected to bring about a sense of calm in the cryptocurrency market. This development is anticipated to stabilize the market and create a more regulated environment for investors. The Clarity Act aims to provide clear guidelines and regulations for the crypto industry, with the potential to impact the market significantly. This move comes at a crucial time as the cryptocurrency market has been experiencing volatility, with Bitcoin reaching a high of $60,000 recently. Investors are hopeful that the Clarity Act will bring more stability and confidence to the market, leading to potentially positive outcomes for the industry.

data.description: Bitcoin and multiple alternative cryptocurrencies are currently experiencing a relief rally, indicating that the buyers are striving to establish a higher low in the market.

data.title: Dr. Austin Harris of NeuroRelief Ketamine & Infusion Therapy in Los Angeles is advancing personalized treatment for mental health, chronic pain, and substance use recovery.
data.description: A medical professional, Austin Harris MD, is making significant strides in revolutionizing the treatment approach for mental health, chronic pain, and substance use recovery. Serving as the Medical Director of NeuroRelief Ketamine & Infusion Therapy in Sherman Oaks, California, Dr. Harris, a board-certified cardiothoracic anesthesiologist, is at the forefront of this innovative transformation. His expertise and guidance are setting new benchmarks in this field as of February 13, 2026.

data.title: Dr. Haeger Scholarship offers national opportunity for undergrad med students. Founded by Dr. Haeger, now accepting applications.
data.description: In Brewster, Washington, on February 13, 2026, the Dr. Eric Haeger Scholarship for Future Doctors, established by Dr. Eric Haeger, has opened its application process for undergraduate students dedicated to a medical career. This scholarship aims to assist students who exhibit a profound dedication to entering the field of medicine.

data.description: On February 13, 2026, in Jersey City, New Jersey, Ambac Assurance Corporation, an insurance company overseeing financial guarantees and complex liabilities, along with its subsidiaries and affiliates, announced their services. Ambac specializes in insurance-focused asset management and offers a range of products to manage portfolios.

data.title: Rivian's stock jumped 30% on upgrades, gearing up to launch R2 as a rival to Tesla Model Y.
data.description: Rivian experienced a significant increase in stock price before the opening of the market on Friday following multiple upgrades. The surge of 30% came as analysts expressed optimism for the company's new electric vehicle, the R2, which is set to compete with Tesla's Model Y. This sudden rise in stock value reflects the growing attention and positive outlook towards Rivian as it prepares to launch its latest product.

data.title: TJX Cos stock receives upgrade in Relative Strength Rating due to improving price performance.
data.description: TJX Companies is displaying stronger price performance, resulting in an upgrade to its IBD Relative Strength Rating. The company's stock has received a boost in its relative strength rating.

data.title: NetScout Systems' stock receives Relative Strength Rating upgrade, indicating improved technical performance. Will the trend persist?
data.description: NetScout Systems has received a boost in its Relative Strength Rating, indicating an improvement in its technical performance. Investors are now wondering if this positive momentum will be sustained. This development was reported in an article titled "NetScout Systems Stock Gets Relative Strength Rating Lift" on Investor's Business Daily.
